title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
summary: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa presents experience building and scaling OG Assist, an AI agent product serving thousands of state and local governments. The session covers core components including agent loops, A2A protocol, Effect-TS implementation, evals and feedback systems, long context handling, monitoring, tool building, and enterprise patterns using Claude and Cursor.
writeup: Gabe De Mesa of OpenGov walks through building and scaling OG Assist, an AI agent product deployed across thousands of state and local governments. The talk breaks down the system's core components: agent loops, the A2A protocol, an Effect-TS implementation, evals and feedback systems, long-context handling, monitoring and observability, and tool building. It also covers enterprise patterns drawn from working with Claude and Cursor. The value is a concrete account of taking agents from prototype to production at scale in a regulated public-sector setting.
